name: Río Ebro to Mirador de Azagra – circular from Azagra
name_original: Río Ebro a Mirador de Azagra – circular desde Azagra
region_intro: Hiking around Azagra offers a variety of trails along the Río Ebro and to local viewpoints. The region is characterized by its riverine landscapes and gentle elevation changes, providing accessible outdoor experiences. Trails often follow the Ebro's banks, with some routes ascending to miradors for broader views of the surrounding area. The terrain is generally flat to gently undulating, making it suitable for various skill levels. Best hiking trails around Azagra The most popular hiking route is Ebro Nature Trail (GR 99): San Adrián Loop, a 3.9 miles (6.3 km) trail that takes 1 hour 35 minutes to complete. This easy route follows the GR 99 along the Ebro river. Another top favourite among local hikers is Río Ebro to Mirador de Azagra – circular from Azagra, a moderate 7.6 miles (12.2 km) path. This circular trail combines riverside sections with an ascent to a local viewpoint. Local hikers also love the San Adrián by the Río Ebro — circular, a 3.7 miles (5.9 km) trail leading through riverine landscapes, often completed in about 1 hour 30 minutes. Hiking around Azagra is defined by river paths along the Ebro and routes to local viewpoints. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy riverside strolls to moderate circular trails. The routes in Azagra are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.3 stars from over 20 reviews. More than 170 hikers have used komoot to explore Azagra's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available around Azagra?
A: There are over 35 hiking trails around Azagra, offering a variety of options for different skill levels and preferences. The region provides a good mix of easy riverside strolls and more challenging routes with viewpoints.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on hikes around Azagra?
A: Hiking around Azagra is characterized by riverine landscapes along the Río Ebro and routes that ascend to local viewpoints. The terrain is generally flat to gently undulating, making it accessible for various skill levels, with some trails featuring moderate elevation changes.

Q: Are there easy hiking trails suitable for beginners in Azagra?
A: Yes, Azagra offers many easy trails perfect for beginners. Over 20 routes are classified as easy, often following the banks of the Ebro river. An excellent option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1246191784/san-adrian-by-the-rio-ebro-circular" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;San Adrián by the Río Ebro — circular&lt;/a&gt;, which is about 3.7 miles (5.9 km) long and takes around 1 hour 30 minutes.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes in Azagra?
A: Yes, Azagra features several circular hiking routes. A popular choice is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1111125107/rio-ebro-to-mirador-de-azagra-circular-from-azagra" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Río Ebro to Mirador de Azagra – circular from Azagra&lt;/a&gt;, a moderate 7.6-mile (12.2 km) path that combines riverside sections with an ascent to a local viewpoint. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/17544931" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Azagra loop from Azagra&lt;/a&gt;, an easy 3.4-mile (5.5 km) route.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the hiking trails in Azagra?
A: Most trails around Azagra are suitable for dogs, especially the riverside paths which offer open spaces. It's always recommended to keep your dog on a leash, especially in areas with wildlife or near agricultural land, and to carry water for both yourself and your pet.

Q: When is the best time of year to hike in Azagra?
A: The best time to hike in Azagra is typically during spring (April-May) and autumn (September-October) when temperatures are mild and the natural scenery along the Ebro river is particularly vibrant. Summer can be hot, while winter offers cooler, sometimes foggy, conditions, but trails remain accessible.

Q: Are there any trails with good viewpoints in Azagra?
A: Yes, some trails in Azagra lead to elevated spots offering panoramic views. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1111125107/rio-ebro-to-mirador-de-azagra-circular-from-azagra" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Río Ebro to Mirador de Azagra – circular from Azagra&lt;/a&gt; specifically includes an ascent to a local mirador, providing broader views of the surrounding area and the river.

Q: What interesting landmarks or attractions can I see near the hiking trails?
A: While hiking around Azagra, you can explore several nearby attractions. Consider visiting &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2210948" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Marcilla Castle&lt;/a&gt;, a historic fortress, or the tranquil &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5982899" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Perdiguero Reservoir&lt;/a&gt;. The nearby towns of &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5692290" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;San Adrián&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5304112" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Funes&lt;/a&gt; also offer local charm.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about hiking in Azagra?
A: The hiking trails in Azagra are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.3 stars from over 20 reviews. Hikers often praise the accessible riverine landscapes, the gentle nature of the trails, and the opportunity to combine riverside walks with ascents to scenic viewpoints.

Q: Are there any longer, more challenging hikes around Azagra?
A: For those seeking a longer challenge, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1246232058/san-adrian-and-camino-natural-del-ebro-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;San Adrián and Camino Natural del Ebro Loop&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 13-mile (21 km) route. This trail offers a more extensive exploration of the Ebro's natural path and surrounding areas, with greater elevation changes.

Q: Is there public transport access to the hiking areas in Azagra?
A: Azagra is a well-connected town, and local bus services can provide access to the town center, from where many trails are easily reachable on foot. For specific trailheads outside the town, a car might be more convenient, but the main river paths are often accessible directly from Azagra.

Q: Are there places to eat or drink near the hiking trails in Azagra?
A: Azagra itself has various local establishments where you can find refreshments and meals before or after your hike. While there might not be cafes directly on every trail, the town center is usually a short distance from the main trailheads along the Río Ebro.
tips: I'm not sure if this is an official viewpoint or not, but it's a good place to get a view of the city. It is also a good place to watch storks who like to perch and build nests on top of buildings.

Azagra is a small town of almost 4,000 inhabitants. It has several interesting churches and buildings to visit. On one side of the city you have a small mountain and on the other side fields that extend to the Ebro river.

waypoints.name: Viewpoint of Azagra and Stork Nests
